codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the stated bug: when map_channel_page fails after both handles are inserted into the handle table, the handles are now explicitly closed before returning the error. Three failure paths are covered (shared_pages returns None, first map fails, second map fails). TDD cycle was properly followed — tests failed before fix, passed after. The kernel code is clean, follows existing patterns, and the test models accurately reflect the kernel's handle-cleanup behavior.

issues.description: sys_channel_create_fixed is functionally identical to sys_channel_create_model after the model was updated to match the fix. Three tests use _fixed and three use _model, but both functions have the same logic. This is dead code duplication. Consider removing _fixed and having all tests use the single model function.

The test model does not cover the shared_pages() failure path that was also fixed in the kernel (syscall.rs lines 268-274). The model jumps straight to simulating map_channel_page results, missing the earlier failure point. A test with a simulated shared_pages failure would improve coverage.

At commit 2cd1df1, the first map_channel_page return value (the virtual address) is discarded via .is_none(), so if the second mapping fails, the first page's PTE cannot be rolled back. This was correctly addressed by the subsequent commit 9d83ab7 (a separate feature), so it is not blocking for this review. Noting for completeness.

sharedStateObservations.description: The kernel-auditor skill procedure (Step 5) says 'Write a test that demonstrates the bug (the test must FAIL before the fix)'. The worker followed this correctly — the transcript shows tests failing before fix and passing after. However, after applying the fix, the worker updated the 'buggy model' to match the fixed code, resulting in two identical model functions. The skill could note that once the model is updated to match the fix, any separate 'fixed' model function becomes redundant and should be removed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: channel_create_leak.rs: sys_channel_create_model (line 46) and sys_channel_create_fixed (line 99) have identical logic after the model update. Transcript shows the explicit Edit step that made them identical.

issues.description: sys_channel_create_model and sys_channel_create_fixed are functionally identical after the model was updated to match the fix — dead code duplication in the test file.
No test coverage for the shared_pages() failure path (kernel syscall.rs lines 268-274), which was also fixed in this commit but not modeled in the test.
First map_channel_page VA is discarded in this commit, preventing PTE rollback on second map failure. Addressed by subsequent commit 9d83ab7, so not blocking here.

summary: The fix correctly closes both handle table slots when map_channel_page fails in sys_channel_create, addressing the described resource leak. TDD was properly followed with 6 tests, all verification steps passed (949 tests, clean build). Two non-blocking issues: redundant identical model functions in tests, and missing test coverage for the shared_pages() failure path.
